what_it_does: This file is a channel-memory attachment record for the design-assets channel; it registers an image attachment by id, type, target URI, and added timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter block with no body content
Added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset
standouts: References a specific design image asset via an image:// URI
Acts as a ledger entry linking an attachment id to a channel and an asset target
conventions: Target uses a custom image:// scheme
concerns: No human-readable title, caption, or description for the image
Target URI cannot be resolved or validated from within this file alone
No provenance fields such as added_by or source
notes: A minimal timestamped registration stub that links a design-assets channel to an image attachment target.
questions: What visual content does Feature_7_Markup represent?
Where is the actual image file stored relative to the image:// URI?
Who or what process added this attachment?
Is this attachment still current or has it been superseded?
intention_signals: It appears intended to record that a specific image asset was attached to the design-assets channel so it can be referenced, audited, and retrieved later.
contribution: Provides a stable attachment identifier and a timestamped link between a channel and a design image asset, supporting lookup and audit of when the asset was added.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a channel-memory attachment record for the design-assets channel, registering an image attachment (att-8e44ff74) that targets image://design-assets/icons/Feature_7_Markup with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to catalog and timestamp an image asset attachment in a design-assets channel ledger.
